sections.title: Limitations / Miscellaneous
sections.body:
he PF / PM task allocation shall be at the discretion of and assigned by the PIC. When making this assignment the
PIC shall take into consideration the fact that some manoeuvres (e.g. the TELFS emergency procedure) have only been
trained in the simulator as PF by PICs.

Jeppesen Charts 11-1 and 11-2 (only available on installed EFB) are replaced by Jeppesen (NetJets) tailored chart 11-
01 (only available on the Portable EFB). Display of the 11-1 chart on an installed EFB may aid to situational awareness,
however, the primary reference should remain the Jeppesen (NetJets) tailored chart 11-01 on the Portable EFB.

Jeppesen (NetJets) tailored charts will not appear on installed EFB so reference to the Portable EFB is essential when
operating to LOWI.

PF shall hold a current AHQ.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
10-10-2P2

-

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
nnsbruck is a mountain airport located in Western Austria. It is situated in the Inn valley oriented East-West. Expect
the possibility of rapidly changing weather conditions throughout the year, particularly during the winter. LOWI gets
very busy during ski season weekends. Expect ATC slot restrictions and tight coordination.

Overview of the Airport

lines: nnsbruck is a mountain airport located in Western Austria. It is situated in the Inn valley oriented East-West. Expect
the possibility of rapidly changing weather conditions throughout the year, particularly during the winter. LOWI gets
very busy during ski season weekends. Expect ATC slot restrictions and tight coordination.

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
EATHER
Whenever marginal weather conditions are to be expected for departure, crews should contact Innsbruck Forecast
(Tel. +43 5 1703 4643) or Innsbruck Tower (Tel. +43 5 1703 6612) to obtain actual weather reports. Weather
information including web-cam pictures may also be viewed at www.innsbruck-airport.com.
Expect Foehn wind when LIPB QNH is 6 hPa greater than LOWI QNH.
During FOEHN conditions (wind 100-180, 15-25 KTS, gusts 30-50 KTS), possible vertical and horizontal windshear with
moderate to severe turbulence, especially over the city, to the south of the city and over the Inn river short of runway
08, below 5000 feet.

ARRIVAL CTOTS
When the weather is marginal, arrivals into Innsbruck may be regulated by use of CTOT at departure aerodrome, due
to ‘special arrival procedure’ to prevent the departure of aircraft that have a low chance of making a successful
approach at Innsbruck.
Due to the various special approvals for lower minima, this is not always applied 100% correctly - operators who
believe they should be included in the approved flight list, should contact Innsbruck Supervisor on +43 5 1703 6610.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
10-10-2P7

-
COLD TEMPERATURE CORRECTIONS
Correction of Minimum Radar Vectoring Altitude is done by ATC, all other corrections are the responsibility of the
pilot – ATC and the ATIS may provide some information on required cold temperature corrections.

sections.title: Arrival procedures
sections.body:
.1 GENERAL
Refer to Jeppesen.

9.2. ARRIVALS RWY 26
See the table Arrivals Runway 26 under 6. WEATHER / AIRPORT LIMITATIONS for a list of arrivals Runway 26 with
applicable limitations, see below operating instructions for RWY 26 arrivals:
• 9.2.1. RWY 26 - NETJETS LOC DME EAST
• 9.2.2. RNP E RWY 26 (LPV)
• 9.5. VISUAL APPROACH
See also 8. Operational Procedures - General for further info on Weather, Arrival CTOTs, and Cold Temperature
Corrections.

Final RWY 26

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
10-10-2P8

-
9.2.1 RWY 26 - NETJETS LOC DME EAST - CHART 11-01
ARRIVAL GENERAL: See also 8. Operational Procedures - General for further info on Weather, Arrival CTOTs, and Cold
Temperature Corrections.

MINIMA (NIGHT APPROVED):
• NetJets is approved to use lower minima than the standard LOC DME EAST (Jeppesen chart 11-1).
• Correct procedure and minima are shown on Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 11-01.
• Minima depends on missed approach climb gradient, the lowest minima is 2900 ft (1006 ft) / CAT B - 2800 m
/ CAT C 3700m and requires a 6% missed approach climb gradient.
• Cloud ceiling is required to be at or above the minima.

PROCEDURE & MISSED APPROACH:
The primary reference shall be the Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 11-01 (only available on the iPad EFB).

For aircraft with an installed EFB it is recommended to display the chart 11-1 (only available on the installed EFB) to
aid situational awareness.

Chart 11-1 is identical to the Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 11-01 except that 11-1 is missing the following:
• Lower Minima which NetJets is approved to fly to.
• OEV DME vs ALTITUDE references inside 8.0 OEV.
The missed approach is identical on both charts, so in the event of a missed approach the 11-1 on the installed EFB
may be referenced.

BALKED LANDING:
See Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 11-01A (Note that this procedure requires the conventional aids OEV and OEJ to be
serviceable) or 9.4 VISUAL CIRCUIT (AFTER BALKED LANDING) if a visual circuit is required.

9.2.2 RNP E RWY 26 (LPV) - CHART 12-2
LPV APPROVED AIRCRAFT ONLY
ARRIVAL GENERAL: See also 8. Operational Procedures - General for further info on Weather, Arrival CTOTs, and
Cold Temperature Corrections.

MINIMA (NIGHT APPROVED):
• Minima is available for LPV approved aircraft only (i.e. there is no LNAV or LNAV / VNAV minima).
• Minima depends on missed approach climb gradient, the lowest minima is 3300 ft (1406 ft) / CAT B - 1500
m / CAT C 2400m and requires a 7.1% missed approach climb gradient.

PROCEDURE & MISSED APPROACH: See Jeppesen chart 12-2.

BALKED LANDING:
See Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 11-01A (Note that this procedure requires the conventional aids OEV and OEJ to be
serviceable) or 9.4 VISUAL CIRCUIT (AFTER BALKED LANDING) if a visual circuit is required.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
10-10-2P9

-
9.3. ARRIVALS RWY 08
See the table Arrivals Runway 08 under 6. WEATHER / AIRPORT LIMITATIONS for a list of arrivals Runway 08 with
applicable limitations, see below for operating instructions for RWY 08 arrivals:
• 9.3.1. RNP VISUAL V RWY 08.
• 9.3.2. NETJETS LOC DME EAST to CIRCLING APPROACH RWY 08 .
• 9.5. VISUAL APPROACH.
See also 8. Operational Procedures - General for further info on Weather, Arrival CTOTs, and Cold Temperature
Corrections.

WARNING: FOEHN
With FOEHN Conditions expect the runway in use to be Runway 08 Crews should note that during FOEHN conditions
severe downdraughts may be encountered south of the city - in the event of a balked landing, pilots are advised to
route visually north of the city before picking up the standard balked landing routing.

Final RWY 08

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P10

-
9.3.1 RNP VISUAL V RWY 08 - CHART 12-1
WARNING: FOEHN
ARRIVAL GENERAL: See also 8. Operational Procedures - General for further info on Weather, Arrival CTOTs, and
Cold Temperature Corrections.

MINIMA (DAY ONLY):
• Minima is as shown on Jeppesen Chart 12-1 & copied below.
• CAT B & C -  MDA(H) 7100 ft (5193 ft)
Visibility 5000 m
Ceiling 5200 ft.
• Note the Ceiling requirement.
• LOWI has a ceilometer at WI814 so ATC are able to provide the cloud ceiling at this point on request.
PROCEDURE & MISSED APPROACH:
• See Jeppesen chart 12-1.
• Note that is will be necessary to sequence the missed approach in the FMS in order to continue to receive
guidance in the visual segment following the MAP at WI814.
BALKED LANDING:
If an escape procedure is required, then see Jeppesen Chart 10-3 RWY 08 Initial Departure and Jeppesen (Netjets)
Chart 10-7 Engine Failure Procedure Rwy 08 as applicable or 9.4 VISUAL CIRCUIT (AFTER BALKED LANDING) if a visual
circuit is required.

9.3.2 NETJETS LOC DME EAST TO CIRCLING APPROACH RWY 08 - CHART 11-01 TO 19-10
WARNING: FOEHN
ARRIVAL GENERAL: See also 8. Operational Procedures - General for further info on Weather, Arrival CTOTs, and Cold
Temperature Corrections.

MINIMA (DAY ONLY):
NetJets is approved to use lower minima than the standard LOC DME EAST (Jeppesen chart 11-1), the correct
procedure and minima are shown on Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 11-01 and below.

CAT B - MDA(H) 3700 ft (1793 ft) / Visibility 3000 m.

PROCEDURE & MISSED APPROACH: See Jeppesen (NetJets) 11-01 and Jeppesen chart 19-10 for procedure.

BALKED LANDING:
If an escape procedure is required, then see Jeppesen Chart 10-3 RWY 08 Initial Departure and Jeppesen (Netjets)
Chart 10-7 Engine Failure Procedure RWY 08 as applicable or 9.4 VISUAL CIRCUIT (AFTER BALKED LANDING) if a visual
circuit is required.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P11

-
9.4. VISUAL CIRCUIT (AFTER BALKED LANDING)
MINIMA (DAY ONLY): Minima for a visual circuit after a balked landing is Ceiling 3093 ft / Visibility 5000 m.

PROCEDURE:
If VMC with the required weather minima and a circuit to land is required following a balked landing from either
runway then follow the tracks shown on the visual circuit diagram below.

Visual Circuit (After Balked landing)
9.5 VISUAL APPROACH
ARRIVAL GENERAL: See also 8. Operational Procedures - General for further info on Weather, Arrival CTOTs, and Cold
Temperature Corrections.

VISUAL APPROACH:
When weather is good, ATC may offer a visual approach to either runway, NetJets aircraft may accept such an arrival
only if it would save a significant delay, has been thoroughly briefed in accordance with OM-A 8.3.28.11 VISUAL
APPROACHES UNDER IFR and OM-A 8.3.28.5 POSITIVE IDENTIFICATION OF AERODROMES, and subject to the
following:

MINIMA (DAY ONLY): Visibility 10 km, Ceiling 9100 ft.

PROCEDURE & MISSED APPROACH:
The route to be used for the approach and missed approach shall be planned and briefed prior to commencing the
approach (consider planning to intercept the final part of an appropriate instrument approach where feasible).

BALKED LANDING:
A balked landing procedure appropriate to the runway in use shall be selected and briefed prior to commencing the
approach.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P12

-

sections.title: Departure procedures
sections.body:
0.1 GENERAL
SLOT ADHERENCE
• Monitor TWR freq. at latest 15 min prior CTOT.
• Inform ATC that you are able to comply with CTOT as soon as possible.
• If unable - inform ATC that:
o you need a SLOT - extension of maximum 15 min; or
o you need a delay with more than 15 min.

RUNWAY IN USE VS PLANNED RUNWAY
• Check the ATIS early.
• When the RWY in use stated is the same as your planned SIDRWY: just plan for the SID you want to use, no
need to call ATC for SID or RWY in use.
• Upon startup request, state the requested SID ‘Fraction XXX request startup via SID XXX’.
• When you are unable to depart on the stated RWY in use, advise ATC together with the estimate to be fully
ready for startup and which SID is needed (ATC prefer KPT5J when you need RWY08 and RWY26 is in use).

LINE-UP PROCEDURES
• ATC expects you will backtrack for full length, there is no need to request this.
• In case of inbound traffic you will be asked if you are able to depart within 3 minutes, before reaching the
holding point – 3 minutes from line-up to take-off means next inbound is about 10nm final with speed 160kt.

10.2. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 08
See also 10.1 General for info on Slot adherance, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up Procedures and also 11.
Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.
See the table Departure Runway 08 under 6. WEATHER / AIRPORT LIMITATIONS for a list of departures Runway 08
with applicable limitations, see below for operating instructions for the following departures:
• 10.2.1. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 08 - RTT 2Q, RTT 3J & all SIDs following the RWY 08 INITIAL DEPARTURE.
• 10.2.2. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 08 - KPT 5J, ADILO 2J & BRENO 2J.
• 10.4. VISUAL DEPARTURE TO XXXXX.

WARNING: FOEHN
During FOEHN conditions severe downdraughts may be encountered south of the city, when able to do so in VMC
pilots should proceed visually north of the city before picking up the standard routing - however if departing in IMC
conditions then the SID routing must be accurately adhered to.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P13

-
10.2.1 DEPARTURE RUNWAY 08 - RTT 2Q, RTT 3J & ALL SIDS FOLLOWING THE RWY 08 INITIAL DEPARTURE
Departure General: See also 10.1 General for info on Slot adherence, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up
Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.

WARNING: FOEHN

MINIMA (NIGHT APPROVED): RVR 150 m.

PERFORMANCE DATA:
• If planned take-off mass permits then use PDCS chart ‘08DP5’.
• If required for an increase in take-off mass then use PDCS chart ‘08DP4’.
Note: for some aircraft types ‘08DP5’ may permit a higher take-off mass than ‘08DP4’.

DEPARTURE ROUTE: As per SID.

CONTINGENCY:
• If using PDCS chart ‘08DP5’: as per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7C ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 08
(STRAIGHT CLIMB).
• If using PDCS chart ‘08DP4’: as per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7 ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 08
(RACETRACK).

10.2.2 DEPARTURE RUNWAY 08 - KPT 5J, ADILO 2J & BRENO 2J
WARNING: FOEHN
DEPARTURE GENERAL: See also 10.1 General for info on Slot adherance, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up
Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.

MINIMA (NIGHT APPROVED): RVR 150 m.

PERFORMANCE DATA: As per PDCS chart ‘08DP4’.

DEPARTURE ROUTE: As per SID.

CONTINGENCY: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7 ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 08 (RACETRACK).

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P14

-
10.3. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26
See also 10.1. General for info on Slot adherence, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up Procedures, and also

lines: Monitor TWR freq. at latest 15 min prior CTOT.
Inform ATC that you are able to comply with CTOT as soon as possible.
o you need a SLOT - extension of maximum 15 min; or
o you need a delay with more than 15 min.
RUNWAY IN USE VS PLANNED RUNWAY
When the RWY in use stated is the same as your planned SIDRWY: just plan for the SID you want to use, no
need to call ATC for SID or RWY in use.
Upon startup request, state the requested SID ‘Fraction XXX request startup via SID XXX’.
When you are unable to depart on the stated RWY in use, advise ATC together with the estimate to be fully
ready for startup and which SID is needed (ATC prefer KPT5J when you need RWY08 and RWY26 is in use).
ATC expects you will backtrack for full length, there is no need to request this.
In case of inbound traffic you will be asked if you are able to depart within 3 minutes, before reaching the
holding point – 3 minutes from line-up to take-off means next inbound is about 10nm final with speed 160kt.
See also 10.1 General for info on Slot adherance, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up Procedures and also 11.
Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.
See the table Departure Runway 08 under 6. WEATHER / AIRPORT LIMITATIONS for a list of departures Runway 08
with applicable limitations, see below for operating instructions for the following departures:
10.2.1. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 08 - RTT 2Q, RTT 3J & all SIDs following the RWY 08 INITIAL DEPARTURE.
During FOEHN conditions severe downdraughts may be encountered south of the city, when able to do so in VMC
pilots should proceed visually north of the city before picking up the standard routing - however if departing in IMC
conditions then the SID routing must be accurately adhered to.
10.2.1 DEPARTURE RUNWAY 08 - RTT 2Q, RTT 3J & ALL SIDS FOLLOWING THE RWY 08 INITIAL DEPARTURE
Departure General: See also 10.1 General for info on Slot adherence, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up
Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.
If planned take-off mass permits then use PDCS chart ‘08DP5’.
If required for an increase in take-off mass then use PDCS chart ‘08DP4’.
Note: for some aircraft types ‘08DP5’ may permit a higher take-off mass than ‘08DP4’.
If using PDCS chart ‘08DP5’: as per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7C ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 08
If using PDCS chart ‘08DP4’: as per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7 ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 08
DEPARTURE GENERAL: See also 10.1 General for info on Slot adherance, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up
Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.
PERFORMANCE DATA: As per PDCS chart ‘08DP4’.
CONTINGENCY: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7 ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 08 (RACETRACK).
See also 10.1. General for info on Slot adherence, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up Procedures, and also

sections.title: Ground procedures
sections.body:
ee the table Departure Runway 26 under 6. WEATHER / AIRPORT LIMITATIONS for a list of departures Runway 26
with applicable limitations, see below for operating instructions for the following departures:
• 10.3.1. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 - SID MOGTI 1R.
• 10.3.2. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 - SID MOGTI 3H.
• 10.3.3. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 - OTHER RWY 26 SIDs.
• 10.3.4. INCREASED MASS DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 (TELFS VMC CONTINGENCY).
• 10.4. VISUAL DEPARTURE TO XXXXX.

10.3.1. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 - SID MOGTI 1R - CHART 10-3G1
DEPARTURE GENERAL: See also 10.1 General for info on Slot adherence, Runway in use vs. planned runway, Line-up
Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs. stand.

MINIMA (NIGHT APPROVED): As per text at bottom of Jeppesen 10-3G1 (Ceiling 1300 ft / Visibility 1500 m).

PERFORMANCE DATA: As per PDCS chart '26DP8'.

DEPARTURE ROUTE:
• As per the SID.
• Unlike the MOGTI 3H there is NO visual segment on the MOGTI 1R. It is a fully IMC SID.

DEPARTURE PROCEDURE:
• Follow the FMS guidance accurately.
• Climb using best gradient of climb.

CONTINGENCY:
• Follow the MOGTI 1R SID track.
• Maintain best gradient of climb as per the OM-B / AFM engine failure profile.
• Accelerate as per the PDCS chart ‘26DP8’.
Note: The PDCS chart '26DP8' is a fully IMC contingency procedure, so in the event of an engine failure there is no
requirement to maintain VMC / visual.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P15

-
10.3.2. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 - SID MOGTI 3H - CHART 10-3G
DEPARTURE GENERAL: See also 10.1. General for info on Slot adherence, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up
Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.

MINIMA (NIGHT APPROVED): As per text at bottom of Jeppesen 10-3G (Ceiling 2100 ft / Visibility 5000 m).

PERFORMANCE DATA: As per PDCS chart ‘26DP8’.

DEPARTURE ROUTE:
• As per the SID.
• Visual section of the departure extends only until passed WI505 and established on track 290° to WI506, (and
above 4000 ft).
• This visual requirement is due to obstacles penetrating the outside of the rather large protected airspace that
RNAV-1 procedure design requires during a turn, no visual manoeuvring is required.

DEPARTURE PROCEDURE:
• Follow the FMS guidance accurately both during the visual and IMC sections.
• If the cloud ceiling is below your cleared level, then initially set an altitude that will allow you to remain clear
of cloud but with an absolute minimum of 4000 ft (with the weather at minima the ceiling will be 4007 ft
AMSL).
• Once past WI505 and established on 290° track to WI506 immediately resume climb to your cleared level
using best gradient of climb.

CONTINGENCY:
• Follow the MOGTI 3H SID track.
• Maintaining best gradient of climb as per the OM-B / AFM engine failure profile.
• Accelerate as per the PDCS chart ‘26DP8’.
Note: The PDCS chart ‘26DP8’ is a fully IMC contingency procedure so in the event of an engine failure there is no
requirement to maintain VMC / visual.

THREATS:
-
If cleared to the FL but leveling initially at an Altitude - ensure to set STD when resuming climb to the cleared FL.
-
If you misjudged the cloud ceiling and enter cloud prior to passing WI505 do NOT descend to regain visual
conditions, immediately resume climb using best gradient to your cleared level.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P16

-
10.3.3. DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 - OTHER RWY 26 SIDS
DEPARTURE GENERAL: See also 10.1 General for info on Slot adherence, runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up
Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.

DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 - OTHER RWY 26 SID: BRENO 3H, RTT 4H & RTT 1R & all SIDs following the RWY 26 INITIAL
DEPARTURE Jeppesen chart 10-3A are approved.
• If no speed / bank angle limitations are given on the SID then use Max. 150 KIAS / Min. 25° AOB.

MINIMA (DAY ONLY): Visibility 5000 m, Ceiling 2000 ft.

PERFORMANCE DATA: As per PDCS chart ‘26DP7’.

DEPARTURE ROUTE: As per SID.

CONTINGENCY:
• As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7B ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 26 (RTT).
• Maintain visual until established on the OEJ course 065° then climb (IMC allowed) with best gradient on the OEJ.
Note: There are small differences between the turn points and track for the ‘normal’ and ‘contingency’ procedures as
shown below.

Normal procedure [10-3A]
Contingency procedure [10-7B]

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P17

-
10.3.4. INCREASED MASS DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 (TELFS VMC CONTINGENCY)
DEPARTURE GENERAL: See also 10.1. General for info on Slot adherance, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up
Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.

INCREASED MASS DEPARTURE RUNWAY 26 (TELFS VMC CONTINGENCY):
In the event that the PDCS chart ‘26DP8’, ‘26DP7’ or a departure Runway 08 with a tailwind do not permit the
required take-off mass, then departure using the increased take-off mass from PDCS chart ‘26DP6’ is permitted using
any of the SIDs listed as approved, provided the following is adhered to:

MINIMA (DAY ONLY):
• Visibility 8 km, Ceiling 3000 ft overhead aerodrome.
• Ceiling 3000 ft overhead the ceilometer located at WI814 (in the vicinity of TELFS) when this information is
available.

PERFORMANCE DATA: As per PDCS chart ‘26DP6’.

DEPARTURE ROUTE: As per assigned SID.
• If no speed / bank angle limitations are given on the SID then use Max 150 KIAS / Min 25° AOB.

CONTINGENCY: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7A VMC ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 26 (via TELFS).

TANKERING: This procedure shall not be used to facilitate economic tankering.

THREAT BASED BRIEFING TOPICS:
-
This contingency procedure will require a deviation from the assigned SID following an engine failure, the point
of which this will occur will vary depending on the SID assigned and where an engine failure occurs, it is essential
that all decision points and routings are thoroughly briefed prior to departure.

-
The procedure via TELFs requires crews to remain in the valley maintaining VMC for an extended period, crews
should be aware that the weather further down the valley towards TELFS may differ from the weather overhead
the aerodrome, crew shall ask ATC for the ceiling at WI814 (in the vicinity of TELFS) but be aware that this only
gives cloud ceiling at that specific point and no visibility information.

-
Crew should consider and brief their possible actions in the event the weather further down the valley is worse
than expected, forcing them to be inadvertent IMC during the VMC part of the contingency procedure, this may
include; max gradient climb to MSA, requesting assistance from Innsbruck Radar to avoid terrain, use of aircraft
equipment (e.g. TAWS display, Synthetic Vision etc) to avoid terrain.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P18

-
10.4. VISUAL DEPARTURE TO XXXX
DEPARTURE GENERAL: See also 10.1. General for info on Slot adherance, Runway in use vs planned runway, Line-up
Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.

VISUAL DEPARTURE TO XXXXX: ATC may from either runway offer a visual departure to a given point (e.g. ‘visual
departure to KONIN’), NetJets aircraft may accept such a departure subject to the following:

MINIMA (DAY ONLY): Higher of;
• Visibility 10 km, Ceiling 9100 ft; or
• Such a level that will allow adequate visual terrain clearance towards the given point.

PERFORMANCE DATA: As per the PDCS chart for the runway in use and selected contingency procedure (see below).

DEPARTURE ROUTE:
• There is no pre-defined / published routing for a ‘visual departure’.
• In most cases ATC will expect you to climb along the valley in the direction of take-off until reaching an altitude
where a safe turn maintaining visual separation from terrain can be made towards the given point.
• If there is any ambiguity with regard to the departure routing, ATC must be questioned and the ambiguity
resolved prior to departure.

CONTINGENCY:
• Crew shall select and brief the most appropriate contingency procedure for the runway in use and the given
routing.
• PDCS for the selected contingency procedure shall be used for performance data.

Visibility 8 km, Ceiling 3000 ft overhead aerodrome.
Ceiling 3000 ft overhead the ceilometer located at WI814 (in the vicinity of TELFS) when this information is
PERFORMANCE DATA: As per PDCS chart ‘26DP6’.

CONTINGENCY: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7A VMC ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 26 (via TELFS).

This contingency procedure will require a deviation from the assigned SID following an engine failure, the point
of which this will occur will vary depending on the SID assigned and where an engine failure occurs, it is essential
that all decision points and routings are thoroughly briefed prior to departure.
The procedure via TELFs requires crews to remain in the valley maintaining VMC for an extended period, crews
should be aware that the weather further down the valley towards TELFS may differ from the weather overhead
the aerodrome, crew shall ask ATC for the ceiling at WI814 (in the vicinity of TELFS) but be aware that this only
gives cloud ceiling at that specific point and no visibility information.
Crew should consider and brief their possible actions in the event the weather further down the valley is worse
than expected, forcing them to be inadvertent IMC during the VMC part of the contingency procedure, this may
include; max gradient climb to MSA, requesting assistance from Innsbruck Radar to avoid terrain, use of aircraft
equipment (e.g. TAWS display, Synthetic Vision etc) to avoid terrain.

Procedures and also 11. Ground Procedures for info on pushback vs stand.

Such a level that will allow adequate visual terrain clearance towards the given point.
PERFORMANCE DATA: As per the PDCS chart for the runway in use and selected contingency procedure (see below).

In most cases ATC will expect you to climb along the valley in the direction of take-off until reaching an altitude
where a safe turn maintaining visual separation from terrain can be made towards the given point.

Crew shall select and brief the most appropriate contingency procedure for the runway in use and the given

sections.title: Ground procedures
sections.body:
here are 10 parking stands at LOWI, stands 7-10 are restricted to pushback only for large aircraft such as B737 &
A320, however, smaller aircraft such as NetJets aircraft types may taxi out from these stands if sufficient room is
available. If necessary, a marshaller may be requested from ATC to ensure wingtip clearance during the turn out.

Parking Stands
When turning out of stand (without push back), pilots are asked to turn as soon as possible (not taxi to close to the
terminal) and then use as little thrust as possible during the turn when the engines point at the buildings.

INNSBRUCK, AUSTRIA
INNSBRUCK
28 NOV 2025 10-10-2P19

-

A320, however, smaller aircraft such as NetJets aircraft types may taxi out from these stands if sufficient room is
available. If necessary, a marshaller may be requested from ATC to ensure wingtip clearance during the turn out.
When turning out of stand (without push back), pilots are asked to turn as soon as possible (not taxi to close to the
terminal) and then use as little thrust as possible during the turn when the engines point at the buildings.

sections.title: Changes since the last revision
sections.body:
RNP Y renamed RNP VISUAL V.
- LOC DME WEST procedure removed.
- LOC DME R and RNP Z (AR) chart index corrected.
- Preferred RWY note added to arrival tables.
- Lowest available minima note made a caution note.
- Day/Night limitation made more visible on arrival/departure tables
- OEI reference corrected for RWY26 arrivals (26DP7 instead of 26DP8)
Provide feedback to Lis-OpsTechnical@netjets.com
